nuscale_ch01_0315.title: SRP 9.2.1, Rev 5: Station Service Water SystemII.1 Protection Against Natural Phenomena (GDC 2)
nuscale_ch01_0315.summary: The NuScale Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) is not safety-related or risk-significant and does not provide essential cooling to safety-related structures, systems, and components (SSC). Regulatory criteria such as GDC 2, GDC 4, and GDC 5 are only applicable to the extent that failure of the nonsafety-related SCWS does not adversely affect safety-related SSCs, and the system’s design and layout ensure compliance with these requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0038.title: Main Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0038.summary: The Main Control Room (MCR) is located below grade in the Control Room Building (CRB) and includes various support spaces such as offices, conference rooms, and equipment rooms. It houses control panels for all installed NuScale Power Modules (NPMs), allowing operators to monitor and control multiple reactors using digital control systems that ensure independence between safety and nonsafety systems. The MCR provides comprehensive monitoring, alarms, and controls for efficient plant operation.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including its main systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains how the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) serves as a reference for site-specific reports, clarifies the use of conceptual design information (CDI), and outlines the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements. Security-related details are specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0072.title: Combined License Information Items
nuscale_ch01_0072.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant certified design and the site-specific design provided in the Combined License (COL) application. It distinguishes between conceptual design information (CDI) for non-certified portions and COL information items, which are site-specific responsibilities of the COL applicant. Additionally, it requires COL applicants to provide a list of any departures from the certified design.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the central Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references figures for detailed layouts and notes that certain buildings, like the administration and training building, are outside the scope of this report.

nuscale_ch01_0361.title: DSRS 9.3.4, Rev 0: Chemical and Volume Control System (PWR) (Including Boron Recovery System)II.5 Chemical and Volume Control System Design and Arrangement
nuscale_ch01_0361.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Chemical and Volume Control System (CVCS), including the Boron Recovery System. It states that the CVCS design generally conforms to the applicable DSRS requirements, with partial conformance noted only for criteria related to holdup tank venting and draining, as the NuScale design does not include holdup tanks subject to vacuum conditions. Other referenced sections are either fully met or not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0842.title: SRP 17.5, Rev 1: Quality Assurance Program Description - Design Certification, Early Site Permit and New COL applicantsII.Q Quality Assurance Records
nuscale_ch01_0842.summary: This section confirms that NuScale’s Quality Assurance Program Description for Design Certification, Early Site Permit, and new Combined License (COL) applicants fully conforms to SRP 17.5, Rev 1 requirements regarding Quality Assurance Records. No exceptions or deviations are noted, indicating full compliance with regulatory criteria for this aspect of the quality assurance program.

nuscale_ch01_0594.title: SRP 13.3, Rev 3: Emergency PlanningII.15 Early Site Permit Criteria - Physical Characteristics Unique to Proposed Site
nuscale_ch01_0594.summary: This section states that the criteria regarding physical characteristics unique to the proposed site (SRP 13.3, Rev 3: Emergency Planning II.15) are not applicable to the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, as these requirements pertain only to early site permit applicants. The NuScale report defers responsibility for site-specific emergency planning aspects to the Combined License (COL) applicant referencing the certified design.

edges.description: Section A discusses regulatory requirements for protecting safety-related structures from failures of nonsafety-related systems, while Section B describes the physical location and layout of the Main Control Room and associated spaces, which must be considered in the conceptual site layout to ensure compliance with protection criteria.

Section A specifies regulatory conformance requirements for the site cooling water system's protection against natural phenomena, while Section B provides the general plant layout and descriptions, including the cooling water systems referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses the protection requirements for the site cooling water system in relation to safety-related structures, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design and the site-specific (including site layout) aspects addressed in the COL application, connecting them through the need to define and ensure proper integration of site-specific systems like the SCWS within the overall conceptual site layout.

Section A provides specific details about the location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room, which is one of the facility components generally described in Section B's overall plant layout summary.

Section A describes the specific below-grade location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room within the conceptual site layout, while Section B outlines the interface requirements and boundaries between the certified design (which includes the Main Control Room) and the site-specific design in the COL application, connecting them through the identification and representation of facility structures.

Section A provides a general description of the overall facility, including the conceptual site layout, while Section B references Section A to identify which structures, systems, and components are part of the certified design versus the site-specific design, thus connecting the general layout to regulatory interface requirements.

Section A describes the general arrangement and layout of major plant structures and equipment, which provides the physical context necessary to evaluate how systems like the Station Service Water System (discussed in Section B) are situated and protected against natural phenomena as required by regulatory criteria.

Section A provides the overall site layout including the location of major structures, while Section B specifies the detailed location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room within that layout.
Section A provides specific details about the arrangement and layout of major structures and equipment, which elaborates on the general facility description summarized in Section B.

Both Section A and Section B discuss the physical arrangement and safety classification of systems (CVCS and SCWS) in relation to their location and impact on safety-related structures, which are key elements in explaining the conceptual site layout.

Section A specifies the design and arrangement requirements for systems like the Chemical and Volume Control System, which must be considered in the conceptual site layout described in Section B, where the Main Control Room and associated spaces are located in relation to these systems.

Section A provides specific details about the Chemical and Volume Control System's design and arrangement, which are part of the broader facility systems summarized in Section B's general plant description.

Section A provides the overall site layout and arrangement of major structures, which includes the placement of systems like the Chemical and Volume Control System described in Section B, thereby specifying the physical context for Section B's system design and arrangement.

Section A describes quality assurance program elements (such as records, test control, and equipment handling) that provide the procedural and documentation framework supporting the implementation and verification of site systems like those described in Section B, which addresses the protection of the Station Service Water System against natural phenomena in the context of site layout.

Section B provides a detailed description of the Main Control Room's physical layout and associated spaces, which is an element that must be addressed and controlled under the quality assurance program requirements outlined in Section A for site design and documentation.

Section A outlines the quality assurance program requirements that must be applied to the design and documentation processes described in Section B's general plant description, ensuring that the conceptual site layout and associated systems are developed and maintained according to regulatory standards.

Section A outlines emergency planning criteria related to site physical characteristics, which are directly influenced by the conceptual site layout described in Section B's discussion of the station service water system's protection against natural phenomena.

Section B provides specific details about the physical location and associated spaces of the Main Control Room, which supports Section A's requirement to describe physical characteristics unique to the proposed site as part of the conceptual site layout.

Section B provides a general description of the plant layout and systems, which supports Section A's discussion of emergency planning criteria that depend on the physical characteristics and configuration of the proposed site.

Section A outlines quality assurance requirements for handling, storage, and control of equipment and records, which indirectly support the emergency planning considerations in Section B by ensuring that site layout and equipment are managed to facilitate emergency response, though neither section directly specifies the conceptual site layout.
